summary: Joined EarthLink in August 2001 to be part of the shift from dial-up to always-on broadband DSL, helping customers through service eligibility, coordination with local phone providers, and installation. As my technical depth grew I added Outlook and connectivity troubleshooting to the role — and, on my own time, taught myself scripting and automation with DOS batch files and QBASIC, building personal tools to speed up password resets, account cancellations, and Outlook fixes. This is where my interest in automation and scripting first emerged, a thread that runs through the rest of my career. The role ended when EarthLink's Pasadena HQ call center was outsourced and I was let go with severance.
highlights: Helped customers transition from dial-up to always-on broadband DSL, determining service eligibility by coordinating with local phone providers (ILECs) and guiding them through installation and upgrades.
Provided technical support for Microsoft Outlook and common connectivity issues as my technical depth grew beyond basic account support.

Self-taught scripting and automation using DOS batch files and QBASIC, building personal tools to streamline password resets, account cancellations, and Outlook troubleshooting — the first spark of the automation mindset that would define the rest of my career.
Carried one of the highest call volumes on the team while maintaining service quality, thanks in large part to those self-built efficiency tools.

Worked out of EarthLink's HQ call center in Pasadena — a notably tight-knit, high-morale environment (arcade, pool table, ping pong, an on-site gym) — until the center was outsourced and the role ended in a severance layoff.
